ipa: loh pehkaoˈɾeh eˈhtaŋ kaˈnsaoh
gloss_en: The fishermen are tired.
notes: Caribbean coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (loh, peskaðoɾeh, kansaðoh) and velar coda /n/ [ŋ] in 'están' (hualde2005).

ipa: lah muˈxeɾeh ˈbjeneŋ del ˈmeɾkao
gloss_en: The women come from the market.
notes: Coda /s/→[h] in the plural article and noun; diphthong 'vienen'; velarised final nasal (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

ipa: ˈtu ˈkjeɾeh ˈmah aˈroh koŋ ˈpehkao
gloss_en: Do you want more rice with fish?
notes: Coastal tuteo; aspiration of 'más' [mah] and seseo 'arroz' [aros]; velar nasal in 'con' (hualde2005).

sentence: Hace mucho calor en toda la costa.
ipa: ˈase ˈmutʃo kaˈloɾ eŋ ˈtoa la ˈkohta
gloss_en: It's very hot all along the coast.
notes: Seseo 'hace'; velarised nasal 'en' [eŋ] before a vowel, a Caribbean trait (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

sentence: Los niños juegan en la playa.
ipa: loh ˈniɲoh ˈxweɣaŋ eŋ la plaˈʝa
gloss_en: The children play on the beach.
notes: Yeismo 'playa'; palatal nasal 'niños'; coda /s/ aspiration and velar nasals stack across the clause (hualde2005).

ipa: ˈbamoh a baˈjlaɾ ˈehta ˈnotʃe
gloss_en: We're going to dance tonight.
notes: Aspiration 'vamos' [bamoh], 'esta' [ehta→esta]; vowel sandhi 'vamos a' (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

ipa: ˈehtoh ˈdoh aˈmiɣoh ˈsoŋ koˈhteɲoh
gloss_en: These two friends are from the coast.
notes: Multiple coda /s/→[h] (estoh, doh, amiɣoh); palatal nasal 'costeños'; velar 'son' (hualde2005).

sentence: No tenemos plata para el bus.
ipa: ˈno teˈnemoh ˈplata paɾa el ˈbuh
gloss_en: We don't have money for the bus.
notes: Negation with aspirated 'tenemos' [tenemoh] and final 'bus' [buh]; Colombian 'plata' for money (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

sentence: Las olas rompen contra las piedras.
ipa: lah ˈolah ˈrompeŋ ˈkontɾa lah ˈpjedɾah
gloss_en: The waves break against the rocks.
notes: Aspiration across plural codas; trill [r] 'rompen'; diphthong 'piedras' (hualde2005).

ipa: ˈkuando ˈʝeɣaŋ tuh ˈpɾimoh
gloss_en: When are your cousins arriving?
notes: Yeismo 'llegan'; aspiration 'tus' [tuh]; velar nasal 'llegan' [ʝeɣaŋ] (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

ipa: ˈbwenoh ˈdiah ˈkomo amaneˈsihte
gloss_en: Good morning, how did you sleep?
notes: Caribbean tuteo greeting; coda /s/→[h] 'días' [diah], 'buenos' [bwenoh]; seseo (hualde2005).

ipa: ˈese ˈpehkao eˈhta ˈbjeŋ ˈfɾehko
gloss_en: That fish is nice and fresh.
notes: Aspiration 'fresco'; velar nasal 'bien' [bjeŋ]; diphthong (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

sentence: Los muchachos fueron a la playa.
ipa: loh muˈtʃatʃoh ˈfweɾoŋ a la plaˈʝa
gloss_en: The boys went to the beach.
notes: Coda /s/→[h] across plurals; yeismo 'playa'; velar nasal 'fueron' [fweɾoŋ] (hualde2005).

sentence: Vamos a comer un buen sancocho.
ipa: ˈbamoh a koˈmeɾ uŋ ˈbweŋ saˈnkotʃo
gloss_en: We're going to eat a good stew.
notes: Aspiration 'vamos' [bamoh]; velar nasals 'un/buen'; coastal cuisine (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

ipa: ˈkuantah seˈɾbesah ˈkjeɾeh
gloss_en: How many beers do you want?
notes: Aspiration 'cuántas/cervezas/quieres'; seseo 'cervezas'; diphthong (hualde2005).

ipa: ˈase deˈmasjao kaˈloɾ aˈki
gloss_en: It's way too hot here.
notes: Seseo 'hace'; open coastal register, no coda /s/ to aspirate but /d/ retained here (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

sentence: No consigo dormir con este calor.
ipa: ˈno koˈnsiɣo doˈɾmiɾ koŋ ˈehte kaˈloɾ
gloss_en: I can't sleep in this heat.
notes: Negation; velar nasal 'con' [koŋ]; coda /s/ before consonant in 'este' surfaces retained here (hualde2005).

sentence: Las palmeras se mueven con el viento.
ipa: lah paˈlmeɾah se ˈmweβeŋ koŋ el ˈbjento
gloss_en: The palms sway in the wind.
notes: Aspiration 'las/palmeras'; diphthongs 'mueven/viento'; velar nasal 'con' (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).

ipa: mih ˈpɾimoh ˈbiβeŋ eŋ baraˈnkiʝa
gloss_en: My cousins live in Barranquilla.
notes: Aspiration 'mis' [mih]; yeismo 'Barranquilla'; velar nasal 'en'; Caribbean toponym (hualde2005).

sentence: El vallenato suena en la plaza.
ipa: el baʝeˈnato ˈswena eŋ la ˈplasa
gloss_en: The vallenato plays in the square.
notes: Yeismo 'vallenato'; diphthong 'suena'; seseo 'plaza'; Caribbean music genre (fontanella_de_weinberg1992).
